channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: NIntendo Direct Announced for Tomorrow Morning
item.content:encoded:
Bringing up the rear of major publishers who seem to be hosting their own events this year in the time of year previously known as E3-season, Nintendo announced that it will be hosting a Nintendo Direct on June 9th, at 10 AM Eastern.

What to know about the Nintendo Direct on June 9

The Nintendo Direct on June 9th will begin at 10 AM Eastern (or 7 am Pacific), and will run for 50 minutes showing both Nintendo Switch, and Nintendo Switch 2 games. It will be followed by another 95 minutes of Nintendo Treehouse Live content showcasing gameplay of some of the titles touched on during the Nintendo Direct. For those who view gameplay reviews as king, you may want to hold your horses during the main direct itself, until knowing if the game will be showing a bunch of gameplay shortly afterwards.

You'll be able to watch the Nintendo Direct on Youtube, Twitch, and via the Nintendo Today app.

With this announcement Nintendo becomes the last of the platform holders to announced their summer announcement event. It will put them a week after Sony held their event on June 2nd which showcased Marvel's Wolverine and God of War Laufey. Xbox took the stage yesterday and showed a mixture of titles as well as a new console-exclusivity plan for some titles like Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution, which won't be on PlayStation 5, or Nintendo Switch 2. 

There's also been a richness of independent shows that showcased everything from single developer projects to some of the biggest games in the industry. On Friday was Geoff Keighley's Summer Game Fest which had grand reveals like Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake, and Final Fantasy VII Revelation which will finally be a day and date all platform launch. Following the Xbox showcase was the PC-centric PC Gaming Show which cut down on the antics a bit but really misses Ubisoft and other publishers putting up shows that might compare in that way. It revealed titles like a new Star Trek video game, Stronghold 4, and a Valheim 1.0 release date. 

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/nintendo-direct-announced-for-tomorrow-morning
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: AION 2's Western Release Promises Epic Flying Battles, Hundreds Of Dungeons, And Low Ping For Global Fans
item.content:encoded:
AION 2, the popular Korean MMO from NCSoft, announced this week that its western launch will take place in September 2026. With this, the sequel to the original Aeon, which launched back in 2008, will finally be available to players all over the world.

At Summer Game Fest, I had a chance to sit down and talk with Daniel LaFuente, Director of Operations, Merv Lee Kwai, Executive Publishing Producer for AION 2, and Seung-Uk Baek, the Chief Business Officer for NCSoft, to see some of what the game has to offer and to discuss the western release.

AION 2 is a direct continuation of the events of the first AION. With the release of the original game, it was revealed that there was a lot more that was wanted to be done with AION from when it was originally released, but “the technology wasn’t ready for any of that.”

Now that we’re 13 years later, for NCSoft, that technology is ready for them to create the MMORPG that they always wanted to release. Some big changes to AION 2 don’t just include massive graphical overhauls, but also a “living and breathing world that’s seamless to move through that you can run, fly, and swim through.”

To show us what the game has to offer, we were walked through the character creation process, we were shown a variety of locations across the continent, and we got to see a whole host of mounts and wings, the wings being a core aspect of AION 2’s gameplay. 

Moving quickly through the character customizer, we were treated to views of a large number of preset creations. Customization not only for hair styles and body styles, but you can get really granular with what you want to change. You can get as focused as raising and lowering cheekbones.

Scrolling quickly through the different outfit customization options, we were quickly shown 800+ themed outfit pieces that could be mixed and matched to create unique and stunning outfits.

The Wings in AION are a key part of the experience; every character gets their own set of wings for traversal and in some places, combat. In the original AION, wings were used to glide, and there was some free-flight allowed in certain cities, but it certainly wasn’t something you could do everywhere. Here in AION 2, you can use your wings to fly freely at any time.

Launch Content For AION 2

At launch, AION will have 200 dungeons split across four different types. Sealed Dungeons are the entry-level combat experiences; get in and clear out the enemies before re-emerging with your hard-won loot.

Nightmare is the first instanced dungeons. These are specifically solo-play dungeons, pitting you against monsters and a boss on your own.

For 4 players, you’ll be able to participate in the Expedition dungeons, which are the entry level for cooperative play. For those who want even more, the 8-player raids are called Sanctuary.

For all of these dungeons, every player will earn rewards of some kind. It was important for the design methodology of the game that no matter what, all of the players are being rewarded.

AION 2 falls into a weird space where they’re the sequel to an incredibly popular MMO, but the first AION is still currently available to play. Asking Baek what the incentives might be for those players who have sunk hundreds of hours into AION to move over and essentially start over in AION 2 I was informed that while there isn’t a specific bonus or reward for ‘returning’ players that they were more than sure that all of the gameplay, combat, and graphical upgrades would be more than enough to get players excited to dive into AION 2.

By a somewhat similar sentiment, I was interested to know for those players who were already experiencing AION 2 through VPNs, why they would want to load up a new character on the global version of the game. Similarly, the benefit here is in the gameplay improvement that players will get from not having to play on a high-ping server. Based on the big support that AION 2 plans to have for players across the US, they expect even in remote locations, players to drop to less than 40 ping.

Andrew has written Video Game and Entertainment news, reviews, and guides for 10+ years. As Senior Content Manager, he assists in creating and editing… More about Andrew



https://techraptor.net/gaming/features/aion-2s-western-release-promises-epic-flying-battles-hundreds-of-dungeons-and-low
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
